name: Tloque Nahuaque
description: Tloque Nahuaque, a significant yet enigmatic figure in Aztec cosmology, is often understood as the supreme creator god, representing the abstract principle of unity and the unseen force underlying all existence. His name translates roughly to 'He who is Near, He who is Nigh,' emphasizing his omnipresence and immanence within the world. Unlike other more anthropomorphic deities in the Aztec pantheon, Tloque Nahuaque is considered formless and without a specific image. He is less a character in myths and more a philosophical concept, the ultimate source of power and being. Some scholars believe he represents a pre-existing, more abstract deity concept adopted and integrated into the complex Aztec religious system. His influence is felt in the creation myths and in the maintenance of cosmic order, although his specific actions are rarely detailed. He is associated with the concept of 'Teotl,' the divine energy or essence that permeates everything. While not directly worshipped with temples or sacrifices in the same way as deities like Huitzilopochtli or Tlaloc, his presence is acknowledged and revered in philosophical and theological discourse, representing the profound mystery at the heart of existence. Tloque Nahuaque's role is to embody the potential for creation and destruction, the cyclical nature of life, and the ultimate interconnectedness of all things. His understanding requires contemplation and philosophical reflection, placing him beyond the scope of simple ritualistic devotion.
subtitle: The Unseen, Omnipresent Creator Force

keyMyths.title: The Origin of Teotl
keyMyths.description: Tloque Nahuaque is considered the ultimate source of Teotl, the divine energy that permeates all things. This energy is the foundation of existence and the driving force behind creation.

keyMyths.title: The Abstract Creator
keyMyths.description: Unlike other Aztec deities with defined roles and stories, Tloque Nahuaque represents the abstract concept of creation. He is the potential from which all things arise, not a specific creator figure.

keyMyths.title: Connection to Omeyocan
keyMyths.description: Tloque Nahuaque is sometimes associated with Omeyocan, the highest level of the Aztec cosmos, a realm of duality and origin. This connection reinforces his role as the ultimate source of being.

sources.author: Miguel León-Portilla
sources.description: Explores Aztec philosophy and the concept of Teotl.
